summary: Given her symptoms including paleness along with laboratory values indicating a severely decreased number of healthy erythrocytes coupled with very low haemoglobin concentration; alongside no strong indications suggesting alternative causes like sideroblastic anaemia or thalassaemia based upon available data—iron-deficiency-anemia remains highly probable as primary differential consideration at this stage. To further solidify this conclusion requires additional tests such as serum ferritin measurement among others but initial impression aligns closely enough already favoring iron deficient state leading us toward diagnosing it first before exploring any secondary possibilities thoroughly after confirming through more specific diagnostics if needed later stages when required clinically by evolving
